title: Grastidium elatum
taxonomy: Grastidium elatum belongs to the family Restionaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as restios. Restionaceae is a family of monocots, characterized by their narrow, grass-like leaves and cylindrical stems. The genus Grastidium is further classified within the family Restionaceae, with Grastidium elatum being one of the species within this genus. Grastidium elatum is native to Australia and is typically found in wetlands and other areas with high water tables. Further classification of Grastidium elatum may include its order, class, and division within the plant kingdom.

intro: Grastidium elatum is a perennial herb native to Australia, belonging to the family Apiaceae and commonly known as Australian angelica or Tasmanian angelica. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Grastidium elatum include volatile oils, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Grastidium elatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, flu, and skin conditions.
